column1: Copy these headers into row 1 of the "offerings" tab:

column3: A weekly gathering where we make sound together
column6: Every Thursday, we gather in a circle to create sound together. No experience needed, no wrong notes. Just presence, intention, and the courage to be heard.

column3: Devotional singing for the modern seeker
column6: Ancient chants in a modern context. We sing mantras, bhajans, and devotional songs from various traditions. It's not about religion—it's about surrender through sound.

column3: Bring the circle to your workplace
column6: Team building through music. We bring the Kythera experience to offices, creating spaces for connection outside of meetings and deadlines.
An intimate group of 12 committed practitioners. Weekly sessions, personal guidance, and a deeper dive into the therapeutic aspects of sound.
Children discover the joy of rhythm and sound in a playful, non-competitive environment. No recitals, no grades—just pure musical exploration.
Quarterly retreats to locations around Rajasthan. Three days of intensive practice, silence, and community in nature.

column1: Copy these headers into row 1 of the "testimonials" tab:

column2: I came for the music but stayed for the people. Kythera has given me a community I didn't know I was missing.
As someone who was told they 'couldn't sing' as a child, Kythera helped me reclaim my voice. No judgment, just support.
Our company's Kythera session was the most connected our team has felt in years. Highly recommend for any workplace.

column1: Copy these headers into row 1 of the "faq" tab:

column3: Not at all. Kythera welcomes everyone regardless of background. Many regulars had never sung in a group before.
Just yourself. We provide instruments though you're welcome to bring your own. Water and snacks provided.

column2: Can I visit once to try it out?
column3: Absolutely. Single-session registration available. Many of us started with just one Thursday.
Not at all. While we use practices from various traditions Kythera is secular. All faiths and no faith welcome.
We welcome children ages 5-12. Parents can drop off or stay - whatever makes the child comfortable.
